title: Prompt Injection attack against LLM-integrated Applications
abstract_summary: Introduces HouYi, a black-box prompt injection attack technique tested on 36 real LLM-integrated applications, finding 31 susceptible. Proposes a framework with pre-constructed prompts, context partition induction, and malicious payloads.

title: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
abstract_summary: Proposes a multi-agent defense framework employing specialized LLM agents in coordinated pipelines to detect and neutralize prompt injection attacks in real-time.

title: Prompt Injection Attacks in Large Language Models and AI Agent Systems: A Comprehensive Review
abstract_summary: Comprehensive review of prompt injection vulnerabilities, attack vectors, and defense mechanisms across LLM and AI agent systems.

title: WASP: Benchmarking Web Agent Security Against Prompt Injection Attacks
abstract_summary: Introduces WASP benchmark for evaluating web agent security against prompt injection attacks in real-world scenarios.

title: Indirect Prompt Injections: Are Firewalls All You Need, or Stronger Benchmarks?
abstract_summary: Evaluates whether firewall-based defenses are sufficient against indirect prompt injections or whether stronger benchmarks are needed to assess defense effectiveness.

title: Prompt Injection Attack to Tool Selection in LLM Agents
abstract_summary: Demonstrates prompt injection attacks targeting tool selection mechanisms in LLM agents, showing how adversaries can manipulate which tools an agent invokes.

title: Securing Large Language Models from Prompt Injection Attacks
abstract_summary: Proposes methods for securing LLMs against prompt injection attacks with practical defense mechanisms.

title: Attention Tracker: Detecting Prompt Injection Attacks in LLMs
abstract_summary: Training-free detection method that tracks attention patterns on instructions to detect prompt injection. Observes attention shift from original to injected instruction in important heads.

title: Bypassing Prompt Injection and Jailbreak Detection in LLM Guardrails
abstract_summary: Demonstrates that prompt injection guardrails can be fully evaded using character injection techniques and imperceptible AML evasion attacks while maintaining payload functionality.

title: From prompt injections to protocol exploits: Threats in LLM-powered AI agents workflows
abstract_summary: Maps the threat landscape from prompt injections to protocol-level exploits in LLM-powered AI agent workflows.

title: PromptGuard: A Structured Framework for Injection Resilient Language Models
abstract_summary: Modular four-layer defense framework integrating input gatekeeping, structured prompt formatting, semantic output validation, and adaptive response refinement. Achieves 67% reduction in injection success rate and F1-score of 0.91.

title: Is Cosine-Similarity of Embeddings Really About Similarity?
abstract_summary: Analyzes limitations of cosine similarity for embeddings, showing it is heavily dependent on method and regularization technique, and can be rendered meaningless in certain configurations.

title: Beyond Cosine Similarity: Taming Semantic Drift and Antonym Intrusion
abstract_summary: Addresses the blind spot of neural embeddings that cannot reliably distinguish synonyms from antonyms, proposing methods to tame semantic drift in large-scale synonym graphs.

title: SemScore: Evaluating LLMs with Semantic Similarity
abstract_summary: Proposes SemScore, a metric for evaluating LLM outputs using embeddings and cosine similarity to measure semantic content quality of generated responses.

title: Reasoning before Comparison: LLM-Enhanced Semantic Similarity Metrics for Domain Specialized Text Analysis
abstract_summary: Proposes LLM-enhanced semantic similarity metrics that apply reasoning before comparison for improved domain-specialized text analysis.

title: Advancing Robust and Aligned Measures of Semantic Similarity
venue: UC Berkeley EECS Tech Report EECS-2024-84
abstract_summary: Advances robust and aligned measures of semantic similarity for embedding-based evaluations of language models.

title: Adversarial Preference Learning for Robust LLM Alignment
abstract_summary: Proposes adversarial preference learning to enhance robustness of LLM alignment against adversarial attacks during RLHF training.

title: Safety Alignment Should Be Made More Than Just a Few Tokens Deep
abstract_summary: Shows that RLHF/DPO alignment concentrates behavioral shifts in the first few tokens, creating vulnerabilities to prefilling attacks. Argues for deeper alignment mechanisms.

title: Why Is RLHF Alignment Shallow? A Gradient Analysis
abstract_summary: Provides gradient-level analysis explaining why RLHF alignment is shallow, revealing that safety alignment concentrates in early token positions.

title: A Framework for Mitigating Malicious RLHF Feedback in LLM Training Using Consensus Based Reward
abstract_summary: COBRA framework addresses malicious RLHF feedback by leveraging trusted cohorts and dynamic variance-guided attention mechanisms for reward aggregation in adversarial environments.

title: Adversarial Training of Reward Models
abstract_summary: Incorporates adversarial responses into reward model training to increase robustness against manipulation during RLHF.

title: LLM Misalignment via Adversarial RLHF Platforms
abstract_summary: Demonstrates how adversarial RLHF platforms can induce LLM misalignment through poisoned feedback and data manipulation during fine-tuning.

title: Safety Misalignment Against Large Language Models
abstract_summary: Studies safety misalignment attacks against LLMs, evaluating four attack strategies: direct request, zero-shot rewrites, few-shot examples, and greedy coordinate gradient optimization.

title: Can LLMs Separate Instructions from Data? And What Do We Even Mean by That?
abstract_summary: Defines the separation score as a probability distribution measuring how differently a model behaves when a probe string is executed versus processed as data. Small separation scores indicate vulnerability to prompt injection.

title: Detection Method for Prompt Injection by Integrating Pre-trained Model and Heuristic Feature Engineering
abstract_summary: DMPI-PMHFE: dual-channel feature fusion framework integrating DeBERTa with heuristic feature engineering for prompt injection detection in LLMs.

title: Indirect Prompt Injection in the Wild for LLM Systems
abstract_summary: Studies indirect prompt injection attacks in real-world LLM systems, analyzing how hidden instructions in external content compromise AI agent behavior.

title: A Practical Framework for Evaluating Medical AI Security: Reproducible Assessment of Jailbreaking and Privacy Vulnerabilities
abstract_summary: Practical framework for evaluating medical AI security across clinical specialties, covering jailbreaking and privacy vulnerabilities with reproducible assessment methodology.

title: Towards Safe AI Clinicians: A Comprehensive Study on Large Language Model Jailbreaking in Healthcare
abstract_summary: Comprehensive study of LLM jailbreaking in healthcare settings including role-playing, authority impersonation, and multi-turn manipulation across medical specialties stratified by clinical risk.

title: Vulnerability of Large Language Models to Prompt Injection When Providing Medical Advice
abstract_summary: Demonstrates 94.4% success rate for webhook-simulated prompt injection attacks in medical LLMs, including 91.7% success in extremely high-harm scenarios involving FDA Category X pregnancy drugs.

title: A Longitudinal Analysis of Declining Medical Safety Messaging in Generative AI Models
abstract_summary: Longitudinal analysis showing medical disclaimers in LLM responses fell from 26.3% in 2022 to 0.97% by 2025, documenting erosion of safety mechanisms over time.

title: Jailbreaking Large Language Models: Navigating the Crossroads of Innovation, Ethics, and Health Risks
abstract_summary: Reviews the intersection of LLM jailbreaking with innovation, ethical considerations, and health risks in medical AI applications.

title: An Audit and Analysis of LLM-Assisted Health Misinformation Jailbreaks
abstract_summary: Audits and analyzes how LLM jailbreaks facilitate health misinformation generation, evaluating the scope and impact of compromised medical AI outputs.

title: OpenAI Guardrails Bypass: The Self-Policing LLM Vulnerability
abstract_summary: Demonstrates that if the base model is vulnerable to prompt injections, the judge model used for self-policing will also be vulnerable, undermining guardrail architectures.

title: Investigating the Effectiveness of Continual Fine-Tuning in Defending Against Medical Adversarial Attacks
abstract_summary: Investigates Continual Fine-Tuning (CFT) as a defense against medical adversarial attacks, emphasizing evolving attack evaluation, domain-specific safety alignment, and safety-utility balancing.
